description:
## Goal

Three small cleanups in `src/fetcher/monitoring.cr` that the
code review flagged as nits:

1. **Rename or document `Monitoring.start`** (nit 6).
   After the merge, `Monitoring` has 13 other public methods;
   calling `.start` on it could plausibly mean "start the
   whole monitoring subsystem" rather than "spawn the
   periodic reporter fiber." Consider renaming to
   `Monitoring.start_reporter`, or add a doc comment on
   `start` clarifying the scope. Pre-existing (the old
   `HealthReporter.start` had the same ambiguity) but worth
   fixing now while we're already in the file.

2. **Document the redundant `if QuickHeadlines.shutting_down?`
   in the reporter loop** (nit 8). The reporter loop has:
   ```crystal
   loop do
     break if QuickHeadlines.shutting_down?  # before sleep
     RefreshLoop::InterruptibleSleep.sleep(REPORT_INTERVAL)
     break if QuickHeadlines.shutting_down?  # after sleep
     report_status
   ```
   The second check is defense-in-depth (only meaningful if
   `shutting_down?` becomes true between the sleep returning
   and `report_status` running). Leaving it doesn't hurt, but
   a comment would make the intent clear. Pre-existing.

3. **`REPORT_INTERVAL` constant in the spec** (nit 9). Will
   be covered by the parallel `quickhea-X1` ticket for the
   `Monitoring` spec. Mentioned here for visibility; do not
   duplicate.

## Why

The renaming is the only one with real readability value.
The other two are doc-only. Cheap to do in a single small
PR.

## Acceptance

- `Monitoring.start` either has a doc comment explaining
  its scope, or is renamed to `Monitoring.start_reporter`.
  If renamed, the single call site in `refresh_loop.cr` is
  updated.
- The second `break if QuickHeadlines.shutting_down?` in
  the reporter loop has a one-line comment explaining why
  it's there.
- Full suite passes
- `just nix-build` succeeds
title: Small cleanups in src/fetcher/monitoring.cr
